codeQualityIssues.description: Sections 1, 4, and 5 of stress_alloc_fragmentation_linked_list use uniform corruption bytes (0xAA, 0xEE, 0xFF respectively) for all allocations within each section. This means the corruption check cannot detect overlapping live allocations (both would have the same byte). In contrast, Part C and Part D of stress_alloc_slab_and_mixed correctly use varying patterns via `(op & 0xFF) as u8`. The uniform-byte approach still detects allocator metadata leaking into user data (the primary corruption vector), so this is a minor effectiveness gap, not a correctness bug.

In Section 2 (line ~272), the code shuffles to_free indices with rng2 then immediately sorts them descending for safe swap_remove. The shuffle is dead code since sort overwrites the ordering. Harmless but slightly confusing — either remove the shuffle or add a comment explaining the sort is for swap_remove safety.

The Rng (xorshift64) struct is duplicated identically across stress_alloc_fragmentation.rs, stress_buddy_coalescing.rs, and stress_sched_ctx_concurrent.rs. Could be extracted to a shared test utility module in future. This follows the existing standalone-test-file convention, so no action needed now.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill says 'For stress test features: Design tests that exercise audit findings under pressure. Follow patterns in existing system/user/fuzz/ and system/user/stress/.' but those directories contain bare-metal tests, not host-side test patterns. The next sentence ('New host-side stress tests go in system/test/tests/') is correct but contradicts the 'follow patterns in' guidance. The worker correctly placed tests in system/test/tests/ and flagged this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. Consider clarifying the skill to say: 'Follow patterns in existing system/test/tests/stress_*.rs files for host-side stress tests.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The kernel-auditor skill says ... but the fuzz/stress directories are for bare-metal tests. Host-side stress tests in system/test/tests/ are the correct location per existing patterns. Consider updating the skill to clarify this.' Confirmed by reviewing system/test/tests/stress_buddy_coalescing.rs which uses the same pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md mentions 'Stress tests use seeded RNG for determinism where randomization is needed' but doesn't specify which RNG implementation to use. All stress tests independently implement the same xorshift64 PRNG struct. Consider adding a note: 'Stress tests use a custom xorshift64 PRNG (no external deps) — see existing stress_*.rs files for the pattern.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Identical Rng struct appears in stress_alloc_fragmentation.rs:123, stress_buddy_coalescing.rs:107, stress_sched_ctx_concurrent.rs:19, scheduler_state.rs:493.

summary: Pass. The implementation is 764 lines across 2 test functions covering all 3 validation criteria (VAL-STRESS-001, VAL-STRESS-002, VAL-STRESS-004). Test 1 (linked-list only) has 5 sections exercising many sizes with random free order (seed 42), different-seed fragmentation, worst-case alternating small/large, 5000-op random alloc/free, and slab-class sizes through linked-list fallback. Test 2 (slab+buddy+mixed) has 4 parts: slab exhaustion/growth with buddy backing verification, exhaustion boundary, 1000-op mixed slab/linked-list/buddy interleaving, and 2000-op GlobalAlloc routing. All use seeded deterministic xorshift64 PRNG matching project convention. Corruption detection via byte patterns, coalescing verification after each section, proper Miri ignore annotation, host memory cleanup. Global state sharing concern (slab pages never freed) is correctly handled by splitting tests. Two non-blocking observations: uniform corruption bytes in some sections reduce overlap detection effectiveness, and a dead shuffle-before-sort. Code quality is high, conventions are followed, and the feature fully covers its description and expectedBehavior.
